content:
The more titles I earned, the more my perspective changed. They became just badges on a coat, but the hunger to grow never stopped. 🏛️✨

Four years ago at SRM IST Vadapalani Campus, I wanted every title I could reach. But as I climbed, those ultimate prizes shifted into just a list of badges. The titles stopped being the goal, but my hunger to learn, build, and lead never stopped for a second.

While my official degree comes later, consider this my Unofficial Farewell, a sign-off to the five arenas that built my mindset:

1. The Culmination of Leadership: President, NextGen Intelligence Club :
NIC was the culmination of every lesson I learned across Association of Computer Science Engineers Club, White Hat Hackers Club, Design and Innovation Club, and SRM Vadapalani Model United Nations. Directing our national symposium GENESIS and 24-hour hackathon HACKFORGE taught me that leadership is taking absolute ownership when a chaotic room looks to you for answers.

2. Building From Zero: Chapter Captain & Sole Founder, ArcShift, Microsoft Chapter of SRMVDP: 
Starting a club from scratch is a brutal, uphill battle. Watching Microsoft officially approve us and leading the team to win 3rd Prize Worldwide in the Microsoft AI Projects competition validated that quiet persistence pays off.

3. The Arena of Grit: Captain, Department Sports Team 
You can write elite code, but you can’t fake chemistry on the field. Serving as the overall captain and Coordinator of department sports team and lifting the Overall Men’s Trophy for 4 consecutive years taught me selflessness, you sweat and win as one unit.

4. The Voice of the Campus: Chief Editor (Impressions & Espirito):
Curating our department and university newsletters taught me the humbling responsibility of stepping back from my own projects to spotlight the milestones of thousands of students and faculty.

5. The Digital Anchor: Social Media Committee Head:
Managing our department's digital brand identity was a masterclass in capturing raw campus energy and turning data and analytics into genuine student connection and excitement.

To every junior, teammate, and faculty advisor who shared these rooms with me: thank you. You let a stubborn student test his limits, make mistakes, and build a legacy from the ground up.

And a quiet thank you to the Ramprakash of these past four years, for never getting tired, refusing to let go of the vision, and getting back up every time he fell.

SRM campus, over and out. Next stop: stepping onto the global stage. 🚀

#SRMIST #Leadership #CampusLegacy #StudentBuilder #ArcShift #NIC #Graduation #GrowthMindset #ClassOf2026 #Resilience

content:
The price of being "Best Outgoing Student" isn't just hard work. It’s the choices no one sees. 🏆

4 years. 49+ Professional Certifications. 9.7 CGPA. National Symposia Leadership. Research Patents. 4 Consecutive Sports Championships.

Today, I’m incredibly humbled to share that I have been awarded the Best Outgoing Student (2022–2026) title at SRM Institute of Science and Technology.

People often ask what the "tipping point" was. The truth? It was choosing myself.

When others were seeking ways to goof off or "hang out," I was working toward my goals. I had to make the difficult choice between fitting in and standing out. I chose the latter, even when it meant losing friends along the way. To reach the top 0.01%, you have to be comfortable being the person who works while everyone else is resting.

The Evolution of Leadership: In my first year, I thought being a leader meant taking everyone to the finish line. By my final year, I realized the hard truth: You can only lead those who want to grow.

I stopped trying to carry those who only complained or waited for rewards they didn't work for. As soon as I let go of that weight and focused on those with a growth mindset, I saw myself and my team go higher than ever before.

To the Batch of 2027: 
1️⃣ Work is Entertainment: If you treat your work as a burden, you'll always be tired. If you turn your work into your enjoyment, you'll never "work" a day in your life. 
2️⃣ The 0.0001% Rule: No matter how many times you fail, if there is even a 0.0001% chance of winning, give it every single bit of energy you have. Most people quit at 99%. Don't be most people.

This award isn't just a trophy; it's a testament to 1,320 days of choosing purpose over comfort.

SRM, it’s been an honor.

#BestOutgoingStudent #Leadership #GrowthMindset #SRM #ClassOf2026 #GoogleStudentAmbassador #TechLeadership #WinningMindset #Resilience #StudentSuccess

content:
You can travel the world for tech, but your true legacy is built at home. 🌍🏫

At the recent Hackforge’26 event, I experienced one of the proudest moments of my college journey. The Department of CSE (ETech) at SRM IST Vadapalani Campus honored me with a Dual Shield, recognizing me as the first person on our campus to hold both the Google and Microsoft Student Ambassador titles simultaneously.

This shield carried the weight of two massive milestones: 
🔹 Being selected as one of the Top 6 Google Student Ambassadors in India.
🔹 Securing the 3rd Prize Worldwide in the Microsoft AI Project Cohort with our Arc Shift project.

But on that same stage, I received another award that means just as much to me: an award for my tenure as President of the NextGen Intelligence Club (NIC).

People often ask why, amidst flying to Dubai and Delhi for global summits, I still poured so much energy into organizing campus events.

The answer is simple. I founded NIC. It was the very first leadership responsibility I took on, long before the global titles arrived. What kind of example would I set for my juniors if I abandoned my roots the second a "bigger" opportunity came along?

This year, NIC broke our department record by drawing 1200+ participants to GENESIS'25. We were also the only club on campus to host an official Google-associated event. None of that happens without a core team willing to put in the absolute maximum effort, right down to or sometimes "at"😉 the final minute.

Titles are great, but the communities you build are what last. I might be graduating soon, but I will always be an NIC member at heart.

Thank you to everyone who made this possible. Let’s keep building! 🚀

content:
A good GPA is great. But the most important lessons in college aren’t taught in a lecture hall. 🏆

It is incredibly easy to let college become nothing more than assignments and exams. Yes, maintaining your academics is absolutely necessary, but if you let it completely tie you down, you miss out on the real education. You have to step outside the classroom and explore sports, events, and communities.

When I first stepped onto the college court, I was just a rookie hungry to play. I tasted gold early on in Basketball and Track & Field, but the journey to true leadership is rarely a straight line. In fact, by my second year, I missed the cut for the basketball squad entirely.

That setback stung. But it became the exact fuel I needed. I channeled that energy into leading the Track & Field team, fought my way back onto the basketball roster the following year for another Gold, and learned what it takes to grind through failure.

All of that culminated in my final year when I was trusted with the role of Department Men's Sports Coordinator.

This year wasn't about my individual game. It was about managing an entire ecosystem. I personally headed Basketball and Track, but the true strength of our department was the incredible juniors who stepped up and the dedicated team captains who led their respective sports.

As Michael Jordan said, "Talent wins games, but teamwork and intelligence win championships."

The result? We didn't just win. We secured the Overall Men’s Trophy with a massive 89 points, leaving the runner-up at 51 points.

This 89-point sweep belongs to every single athlete who left everything on the field. A massive shoutout to my counterpart, our Women's Department Coordinator, Sheerin S. Managing the logistics, the pressure, and the immense scale of our department's sports front alongside you was a masterclass in coordination. We promised we'd bring the trophies home, and we delivered! 🙌

Do not just tie yourself to your desk. Step onto the field. Join that club. Take the loss, learn the lesson, and come back to lead the team.

What extracurricular activity shaped your student life the most? Let me know below! 👇

#SportsLeadership #Teamwork #OverallChampions #Basketball #TrackAndField #StudentLeader #Resilience #CollegeLife #WinningMindset #Intramurals

content:
Everyone sees the polished campus posts. Nobody sees the student builder and the team behind the screen. 📱✨

For 3 whole years, right from the start of my second year, I had the immense privilege of managing our college department's social media channels.

At our farewell ceremony, I was incredibly honored to receive the official Social Media Award from the Department of Computer Science and Engineering (Emerging Technologies) at SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Vadapalani Campus (SRM IST Vadapalani Campus).

Although the ceremony happened a few weeks ago, taking a moment to look back at this trophy made me realize that this journey was as much about navigating change as it was about content creation.

My journey started when I was recruited by Dr.Golda Dilip Mam (current HOD of CSE), who was managing the pages under the guidance of then-HOD Dr.Prasanna Devi S Mam. A year later, our department underwent a structural split, and I transitioned to the Department of CSE (Emerging Technologies). There, I was recruited by Dr.Meenakshi KondaiyanRaj Mam to continue this work under the leadership of our current HOD, Chitra Pasupathi Mam. Finally, during my last few months, I had the opportunity to work alongside Nivitha Mam, who currently drives our digital presence.

There were several managerial changes and shifting dynamics along the way, but I made it a priority to adapt to each leadership style and push through the transitions. Managing an institution's digital voice through these changes was a masterclass in:

🔹 Brand Strategy: Figuring out how to tell cohesive stories that make prospective students, parents, and recruiters take us seriously, regardless of internal shifts. 
🔹 Consistency Under Pressure: Keeping content calendars moving seamlessly through department splits, exam weeks, and hackathons. 
🔹 Audience Engagement: Turning data and analytics into real campus community and hype.

We often talk about building personal brands, but the real test of a marketer and leader is whether you can build a community for an organization and keep it thriving through change.

A massive thank you to Dr.Golda Dilip Mam, Dr.Prasanna Devi S Mam, Dr.Meenakshi KondaiyanRaj Mam, Chitra Pasupathi Mam, and Nivetha Mam for trusting my direction through every single transition. And to my peers and the core student design teams who worked late into the night to keep our feeds looking elite, this belongs to us.

Whether it's an enterprise fintech product or a university department page, the goal remains the same: Tell a story so good that people can't help but stop and engage.

What’s the biggest lesson you’ve learned from managing a community or navigating leadership changes? Let's connect in the comments! 👇

content:
Never ignore an opportunity, however small it starts. You never know where it might lead. 🇦🇪🚀

If you had told me a year ago that I would be sitting on the 53rd floor of the JW Marriott Marquis Dubai, overlooking the Museum of the Future, preparing to represent the Google Student Ambassador Program at the world's largest creator summit... I wouldn't have believed you.

But last week, that became my reality.

I was honored to be shortlisted from thousands of competing student ambassadors across India to attend the 1 Billion Followers Summit in Dubai (Fully Sponsored by Google).

Here is what it feels like to step onto a global platform:

🌐 The Scale & The Energy Walking into the summit near the Emirates Towers, the energy was electric. It wasn't just a tech conference; it was a collision of creativity and future-tech. I initially thought I’d be disappointed about not having time to tour Dubai, but the moment I stepped in, I realized the summit was the destination. There was so much to absorb that the city outside faded away.

🧠 Learning from the Giants The panels were eye-opening. I had the chance to witness insights from industry titans and cultural icons like MrBeast, Nuseir Yassin, #TechnicalGuruji, and #SamanthaRuthPrabhu. The biggest takeaway? Authenticity scales. Whether it's a YouTube video or an AI model, the human element is what connects us.

🎬 The AI Film Festival One of the most profound experiences was the AI Film Festival. Watching "Lili" - the winning film, it was a masterclass in storytelling. It stole my heart. Seeing how AI is being used not just to compute, but to evoke emotion, changed my perspective on what we are building. (Honorable mention to "72 Days for Love", incredible work!)

🤝 The Community & Hospitality A massive shoutout to the Google Gemini Studio—the coolest spot at the summit (and the savior with the best coffee! ☕). It was the perfect hub to network with fellow ambassadors and connect with teams from Twitch, Sony, Red Bull, Snapchat, YouTube, and GOOGLE INDIA.

From the breathtaking view at Room B5313 to the intense challenges at the stalls to win swags, the hospitality was world-class.

🚀 The Growth Representing the Google Student Ambassador Program on this international stage has been a catalyst for my personal growth. It pushed me out of my comfort zone, forced me to network with people from different cultures, and showed me that student leadership has no borders.

I am returning to India not just with swags, but with a global perspective and a renewed fire to build, lead, and innovate.

Thank you to Shekar Khosla, Anmol Arora, Brahmi Shah Chatterjee, team Communique India 🇮🇳, Google India and Team for this life-changing opportunity. 

Also thank you to Creators HQ | مقر المؤثرين for the workspace.

#1BillionSummit #Dubai #GoogleStudentAmbassador #GSP #AI #CreatorEconomy #GlobalExposure #Learning #Growth #Travel #StudentLeader #Gemini

content:
Everyone remembers the student who was always on "OD." I wanted them to remember the results, too. 🎓

Throughout my four years at SRM Institute of Science and Technology, I was rarely sitting at my desk. If you ask anyone who knows me, they will tell you I would probably top the leaderboard for the highest number of "On Duty" (OD) days.

Between organizing national-level symposia, running Hackforge, leading the NextGen Intelligence Club, managing  Social Media Committee and looking after the Newsletter team, managing global responsibilities as both a Google and Microsoft Student Ambassador, and ultimately having the honor of meeting Sundar Pichai as one of the Top 6 Ambassadors in India, I practically lived on OD status.

It is incredibly easy to let those extracurriculars become a valid excuse for slipping grades. Early on, I made a quiet promise to myself that the goal was never just to build a resume; it was to prove that you can immerse yourself fully in college life without sacrificing your career trajectory.

Today, that effort is officially on paper. I have received my B.Tech Provisional Degree Certificate in Computer Science (AI & ML) from SRM IST Vadapalani Campus, officially graduating with First Class with Distinction and a 9.68 CGPA.

The late-night study sessions before each exam which sometime is right after a 12-hour event shifts were brutal. But holding this certificate makes every single sacrificed weekend worth it. I learned that balance isn't about splitting your time 50/50, rather it is about giving 100% of your focus to whatever room you are currently standing in.

Never let your ambition outside the classroom become an excuse for mediocrity inside it.

The undergraduate chapter is officially closed. Next up: building the future. 🚀

#Graduation #FirstClassWithDistinction #SRM #BTech #ComputerScience #ArtificialIntelligence #GoogleStudentAmbassador #MicrosoftAmbassador #AcademicExcellence #GrowthMindset #EngineeringLife
